app.name: Azure Developer CLI (azd)
app.one_liner: A developer-centric CLI to build, deploy, and operate Azure applications—especially AI agents—with opinionated templates and built-in Azure best practices.

screens.name: Project Dashboard
screens.purpose: Orchestrate the end-to-end workflow from code to cloud using guided actions aligned with Azure best practices.

rust_commands.name: invoke_ai_agent
rust_commands.purpose: Send arbitrary input to an AI agent via the /invocations API endpoint.

rust_commands.name: provision_infra
rust_commands.purpose: Execute infrastructure provisioning with support for per-layer hooks defined in azure.yaml.

rust_commands.name: run_hooks
rust_commands.purpose: Execute targeted hooks during deployment or provisioning, optionally scoped to a specific layer.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add basic AI agent invocation command
week 1: Implement core CLI scaffolding with non-interactive mode support
week 1: Integrate azure.yaml parsing with layered hook execution
acceptance_tests: User can initialize a new project using 'azd init' and successfully deploy it to Azure without requiring data seeding workarounds.
User can invoke an AI agent using 'azd ai agent invoke' with arbitrary input passed through the /invocations API.
User can run 'azd provision' with per-layer hooks that execute in correct order without manual intervention.
